note: 90s variant of Tim — brown center-parted curtained hair, green plaid flannel open over dark tee/vest, lighter blue jeans. Used ONLY by the time-warp Easter egg. Pixel-art treatment derived from /references/tim-90s-base.png (gpt-image-1 image-to-image edit on /references/tim-avatar-v2@168.png). The laptop-hack core is NOT generated from this character — it uses the separate tim-90s-laptop variant whose base is already-holding-laptop, because the AutoSprite model couldn't render a believable clamshell laptop from a standing-pose base.

spawn-in-arrival.note: Frame 1 dark silhouette of 90s Tim with rim light → progressively colors in from feet upward through frames 2-7 → fully colored 90s Tim (flannel + hair visible) holds → settles into locked Original standing pose. Reads clearly as 'arrival via materialization'. Era-appropriate for the time-warp gag.

save-and-quit-exit.note: From locked Original pose → raises hand → small 3.5" floppy disk pixel-icon materializes above hand → bright save-flash → 90s Tim dissolves into pixel debris that drifts down and clears the frame. Clean, recognizable 'save and quit' read.

crt-tv-arrival.note: Abandoned. Tim never visibly 'emerged from the TV' — he was already standing at frame 1 while a CRT TV materialized around him, got staticky, and shattered. Visually 90s-themed but didn't read as an arrival. Replaced by spawn-in.

crt-power-off-departure.note: Never generated. Job timed out with 'Timed out during reconciliation' error; AutoSprite worker hung. Replaced by save-and-quit.

laptop-hack-v1.note: Abandoned. Laptop shape rendered poorly — flat green rectangle without convincing clamshell geometry. Replaced by laptop-hack generated against the tim-90s-laptop pose variant (see /references/sprites/tim-90s-laptop/index.json).
